sourceMovie.title: PK
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto PK (2014) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Robot & Frank
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Robot & Frank (2012) captures the comedy appeal of PK without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts Robot & Frank at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Men in Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy appeal of PK — that funny edge, the science-fiction — translates cleanly into Men in Black (1997). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Contact
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Contact (1997) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps PK rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Big Sick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Big Sick (2017) is the kind of film PK fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The comedy overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: City Lights
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: City Lights shares PK's gift for comedy — the 1931 film delivers drama without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: People We Meet on Vacation
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes PK compelling — the comedy, the drama — shows up in People We Meet on Vacation (2026) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Toy Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy energy of PK is alive in Toy Story (1995), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Toy Story 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Toy Story 2 (1999) operates in the same comedy as PK, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial (1982) belongs to the same conversation as PK — both anchor their science-fiction in adventure emotion that doesn't let go. The uplifting undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Aliens
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If PK's science-fiction stayed with you, Aliens (1986) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The action through-line is the real common thread between them.

faq.question: What should I watch after PK?
faq.answer: Try Robot & Frank, Men in Black, Contact—each shares DNA with PK while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is PK?
faq.answer: PK (2014) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is PK worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
